name: Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop
name_original: Paseo por Tierra Bobal —  Circular desde Requena hasta Utiel
description: Cycle an easy 19.6-mile loop through the historic Utiel-Requena wine region, passing vineyards, forests, and the Requena Fortress.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Requena traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive vineyards, rolling hills, and river valleys. The region features natural parks like Hoces del Cabriel and Chera-Sot de Chera, offering varied terrain from quiet roads to forest trails. Cyclists can explore routes alongside the Júcar and Magro rivers, which carve impressive canyons, and encounter elevated areas providing panoramic views. Best touring cycling routes around Requena The most popular touring cycling route is Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop, an easy 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This route offers a gentle ride through the local wine region. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Yátova Path – Requena Vineyards loop from El Rebollar, a difficult 23.2 miles (37.3 km) path. This route navigates through vineyards and rolling hills, presenting a challenging ascent. Local touring cyclists also love the Requena Fortress – Castle and walls of Requena loop from Requena, a 28.6 miles (46.1 km) trail leading through historic areas and offering panoramic views, often completed in about 4 hours 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Requena is defined by rolling hills, extensive vineyards, and river canyons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy vineyard loops to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Requena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Requena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes can I find around Requena?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes available around Requena, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored more than 600 touring cycling routes in the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Requena?
A: Requena's touring cycling routes feature diverse terrain, from quiet roads through extensive vineyards and rolling hills to more challenging ascents in elevated areas. You'll also find routes alongside the Júcar and Magro rivers, which carve impressive canyons, and forest trails, especially suitable for gravel biking.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Requena?
A: Yes, Requena offers easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919295143/requena-and-utiel-via-camino-de-la-lana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 19.6-mile (31.6 km) trail that provides a gentle ride through the local wine region.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge can explore routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yátova Path – Requena Vineyards loop from El Rebollar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.2-mile (37.3 km) path navigating through vineyards and rolling hills with a challenging ascent. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena Fortress – Castle and walls of Requena loop from Requena&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.6-mile (46.1 km) trail through historic areas with panoramic views.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Requena area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Requena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919295143/requena-and-utiel-via-camino-de-la-lana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31845367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yátova Path – Requena Vineyards loop from El Rebollar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Requena's routes offer stunning natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can cycle through the renowned Utiel-Requena wine region with its extensive vineyards, explore areas near the Hoces del Cabriel and Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Parks, or discover impressive canyons carved by the Júcar and Magro rivers. The historic Medieval Quarter (La Villa) with its ancient walls and the Castillo de Requena are also accessible by bike.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the touring bike routes around Requena?
A: Yes, the region's rolling hills and elevated areas, such as Pico Ropé and Pico del Tejo, provide numerous opportunities for panoramic views. Many routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena Fortress – Castle and walls of Requena loop from Requena&lt;/a&gt;, are specifically designed to offer breathtaking vistas of the surrounding landscape and historic sites. You can also find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4118508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; near the Embarcaderos Reservoir.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Requena?
A: The touring cycling routes around Requena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from quiet vineyard roads to challenging mountain ascents, and the region's well-developed infrastructure for active tourism.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes in the Requena area will take you past beautiful bodies of water. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buseo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embarcaderos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic spots for a break or a photo opportunity.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Requena?
A: Requena is an excellent destination for touring cycling throughout much of the year. The autumn months are particularly recommended, especially for the 'Wineland Gravel Experience,' when the vineyards display breathtaking colors. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and blooming landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable.

Q: Can I rent a bike in Requena?
A: Yes, bike rentals are available locally in Requena for those who prefer to hire equipment rather than bringing their own. This makes it convenient to explore the region's extensive network of cycling routes.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes?
A: Given Requena's rich wine culture and numerous villages, you'll find various cafes, pubs, and wineries along or near many touring cycling routes. The region is known for its wine tourism, with over 100 wineries offering tours and tastings, providing excellent opportunities for refreshment and local culinary experiences.
tips: The city of Utiel is not only recognized for its good wines but also offers many places of interest to visit, including the Plaza del Sol, the Wine Museum, underground cellars and several churches. You can follow a tourist route through the town center visiting several of these sites or also visit one of the renowned wineries.

The castle is located on the highest hill of the city, in a strategic point. Of Muslim origin, it was built during the 15th century and only the Torre del Homenaje remains standing of the original building.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this loop are not detailed, both Requena and Utiel are towns with general parking facilities. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of these settlements to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is the Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The route primarily uses rural paths and tracks. While dogs are generally permitted on public paths in Spain, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or on busier sections. Be mindful of varied terrain that might be challenging for paws.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The landscape transforms with the seasons, reflecting the life cycle of the vines. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be very hot, and some sections might be less comfortable for cycling.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle along the public paths and sections of the Camino de la Lana that make up this loop. It is freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring both paved and unpaved surfaces. You will encounter rural paths, historical tracks, and footpaths. Some natural trail sections, particularly around areas like El Rebollar, can have stony or loose surfaces, which might be challenging for some touring bikes.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for touring bicycles?
faqs.answer: While the route is generally considered easy with 'not complicated slopes,' the varied terrain includes unpaved, stony, or loose surfaces in some segments. These parts can increase technical difficulty and might be better suited for mountain bikes or gravel bikes. Touring cyclists might need to dismount and push their bikes in certain sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through extensive vineyards, holm oak forests, and Aleppo pine groves. The route connects charming small villages and offers glimpses of traditional Spain, including monumental heritage. Notable highlights include the town of Utiel and the historic Requena Fortress.

faqs.question: What makes this route historically significant?
faqs.answer: This route is part of the ancient Camino de la Lana (Wool Route), a path used for centuries by drovers, wool merchants, and pilgrims heading to Santiago de Compostela. It passes through areas with a rich history, including Roman and Arab influences, offering an authentic experience of traditional Spain.

faqs.question: Can I experience wine tourism along this route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The loop traverses the Utiel-Requena Designation of Origin wine region, which is renowned for its vineyards. This provides an excellent opportunity to experience wine culture firsthand, with possibilities for visits to vineyards and wineries, especially during events like the 'Wineland Cycling Experience'.

faqs.question: How well is the Camino de la Lana marked in this area?
faqs.answer: The Camino de la Lana is generally well-marked in many sections with the familiar yellow arrows and marker posts, which aids in navigation. However, it's always recommended to carry a map or use a GPS device for optimal route finding, especially when integrating with local paths.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop integrates with other local cycling paths in the Utiel-Requena wine region. It also overlaps with sections of official trails such as Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Valencia, Río Magro - Fuente de Santa Catalina, and Camí Natural Turia - Cabriel.
